assumptions: The developer has a supported browser and email address.
No Honeycomb account, team, region, environment, or ingest API key exists yet.

developer_goal: Create a Honeycomb account and open a first observable trace or equivalent first-success signal for a chosen start path.

first_success.outcome: Honeycomb shows a first trace (or the selected path's equivalent first success) from the developer's chosen instrumentation route.
first_success.observable_signal: For the traces candidate, Honeycomb opens a trace view showing spans, durations, and any errors.

route_selection.rule: Select the shortest complete account-to-first-signal route only after start path, telemetry type, and language are fixed.

candidates.name: Applications traces via OpenTelemetry (language-tabbed)
candidates.reason: Official traces guide branches across JavaScript, Python, Java, .NET, Go, Ruby, and others without ranking one language.

candidates.name: Kubernetes start path
candidates.reason: Explicit peer Get Started path for cluster telemetry.

candidates.name: Example applications
candidates.reason: Peer path using pre-instrumented apps with separate repository instructions.

candidates.name: Honeycomb Sandbox / interactive demo
candidates.reason: Explores vendor demo data without creating the developer's instrumentation path; does not require account telemetry send.

required_path.action: Choose US or EU data storage and create a free Honeycomb account and team.

required_fields.label: Account signup fields
required_fields.notes: Traces guide links US/EU signup but does not enumerate form controls.

required_path.observable_result: The developer has a Honeycomb team and can view or copy an ingest API key.

required_path.action: Copy the Honeycomb ingest API key for later instrumentation.

required_fields.label: Ingest API key
required_fields.notes: Shown once at team creation; also available later in Environment Settings.

required_path.observable_result: The API key is available to configure OpenTelemetry export.

required_path.action: Choose an application language path, add OpenTelemetry instrumentation, run the service, generate traffic, and open a trace.

required_fields.label: Language-specific dependencies, config, and run commands

required_path.observable_result: Honeycomb displays a trace waterfall for a generated request.

prerequisites.description: Access to modify and run an application in a local development environment, or willingness to use an official example app.

external_gates.description: Region choice is made before account creation and determines which Honeycomb instance stores data.

unavoidable_waits.description: After the instrumented service emits spans, Honeycomb must ingest them before query and trace views populate.

platform_outcomes.description: Team creation reveals an ingest API key needed to send data.
OpenTelemetry instrumentation builds spans that Honeycomb can query and open as traces.

excluded.reason: Educational content, not a required product action.
Different outcome that does not send the developer's telemetry.

sources.title: Get Started with Traces and Wide Events
Send Data from Applications with OpenTelemetry
evidence_supported: OTLP endpoint and API key headers

uncertainties.question: Which Honeycomb start path, telemetry type, and application language should define the measured route?
uncertainties.impact: Required packages, config files, run commands, and even whether a trace waterfall is the terminal signal all change.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: A corpus decision selecting one Get Started path and language tab, plus a cold-account walkthrough to that path's first success.

uncertainties.question: What exact fields appear on the current US and EU Honeycomb signup forms?
uncertainties.evidence_needed: Dated captures of the US and EU signup pages linked from the traces guide.
